sections.heading: Create a Team
sections.body:
> **Important:** 
>
> Atlas limits the number of users to a maximum of 100 teams per
> project and a maximum of 250 teams per organization.

Atlas CLI

To create one team in your organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas teams create <name> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas teams create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-teams-create).

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Create a Team
sections.body: To add users to your team, see [add team members](edit-team-members).

sections.heading: Create a Team
sections.body:
To create a team using the Atlas UI:

> **Note:** The name must be unique within an organization.

To add existing organization users to the team, click in the
Add Members box and either start typing their Cloud Manager
username or click on the name of a user that appears in
the combo box.

sections.heading: View Teams
sections.body:
Atlas CLI

To list all teams in your organization using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas teams list [options]

```

To return the details for the team you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas teams describe [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas teams list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-teams-list) and
[atlas teams describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-teams-describe).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

Atlas UI

sections.heading: View Teams
sections.body:
To view your teams using the Atlas UI:

Your teams display.

sections.heading: Add Team Members
sections.body:
> **Important:** 
>
> Atlas limits Atlas user membership to a maximum of 250
> Atlas users per team.

Atlas CLI

To add one user to the team you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas teams users add <userId>... [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas teams users add](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-teams-users-add).

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Add Team Members
sections.body:
To add team members using the Atlas UI:

- Click Add Members.

- Type the name or email of the user from the combo box.

  You can add users that are part of the organization or
  users that have been sent an invitation to join the organization.

- Click Add Members.

sections.heading: Remove Team Members
sections.body:
Atlas CLI

To delete one user from the team you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas teams users delete <userId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas teams users delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-teams-users-delete).

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Remove Team Members
sections.body:
To remove team members using the Atlas UI:

Click  to the right of the user you want to remove
from a team.

Removing a member from the team removes the user's project
assignments granted by the team membership.

If a user is assigned to a project through both a team and
individual assignment, removing the user from a team does not
remove the user's assignment to that project.

sections.heading: Rename a Team
sections.body:
> **Note:** 
>
> Atlas CLI Limitation
>
> You can't rename a team using the Atlas CLI.

For the team you want to rename:

- Click the ellipsis (`...`) button under the Actions
  column.

- Click Rename Team.

- Enter a new name for the team.

  The team name must be unique within the organization.

- Click Rename Team.

sections.heading: Delete a Team
sections.body:
Atlas CLI

To delete one team from your organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas teams delete <teamId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas teams delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-teams-delete).

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Delete a Team
sections.body:
To delete a team using the Atlas UI:

For the team you want to delete:

- Click the ellipsis (`...`) button under the Actions
  column.

- Click Delete Team.

- Confirm that you wish to proceed with team deletion.

  For users belonging to the team, deleting a team removes the
  users' project assignments granted by that team membership.

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.body:
For the organization users in a team to have access to a project, you
must add the team to the project. To add teams to a project or edit
team roles, see [project-level](manage-project-access).
